youtube_items.title: [YT] Ending Trash Pollution by Reusing all Materials in a Circular ...
youtube_items.summary: California has a trash problem. Even after recycling billions of products, we still throw out 40 million tons of garbage a year, turning limited materials into pollution that piles up in land fills and in our streets, rivers and ocean. The solution is to stop treating our waste like trash. As our population grows, we can keep our convenient lives by reusing materials in a circular economy. Cutting plastic pollution by having industry use less plastic and recycled the rest. There's about 1.3 1.4 million pounds of trash in this yard that would have ended up in our landfills. raw_items.title: Lytx’s 2026 Road Safety Report Highlights Emerging Fleet Risks
raw_items.summary: According to the 2026 Road Safety report from Lytx, severe collisions in 2025 declined, but overall crash rates continue to rise due to inattentive driving, handheld device use, and operational pressures. To gain more insights on the report, Waste360 caught up with Dave Riordan, chief customer officer at Lytx, for a Q&A.

raw_items.title: GFL Posts Record Q1 Margin, Raises Outlook
raw_items.summary: GFL Environmental reported first-quarter 2026 results showing steady revenue growth and enough momentum to lift its full-year outlook. Revenue, adjusted EBITDA, and Adjusted Free Cash Flow all came in ahead of expectations.

raw_items.title: Jerseyville, IL Reaches Recycling Policies Agreement with County
raw_items.summary: Jerseyville and Jersey County, Ill., have approved new recycling partnerships for a new recycling program in the area.

raw_items.title: Denton County Dumpster Company Rebrands, Expands to 23 North Texas Cities
raw_items.summary: Iron Eagle Dumpster Rentals, a family-owned roll-off dumpster company in Denton County, Texas, has rebranded from FreedomTX Dumpster Rentals and expanded delivery to 23 cities across North DFW.

raw_items.title: Plastic pollution
raw_items.summary: Plastic pollution is the accumulation of plastic objects and particles (e.g. plastic bottles, bags and microbeads) in the Earth's environment that adversely affects humans, wildlife and their habitat. Plastics that act as pollutants are categorized by size into micro-, meso-, or macro debris. Plastics are inexpensive and durable, making them very adaptable for different uses; as a result, manufact

raw_items.title: Plastic Pollution - Our World in Data
raw_items.summary: Plastic has added much value to our lives: it's a cheap, versatile, and sterile material used in various applications, including construction, home appliances, medical instruments, and food packaging. However, when plastic waste is mismanaged — not recycled, incinerated, or kept in sealed landfills — it becomes an environmental pollutant.

raw_items.title: Recycling
raw_items.summary: Recycling is the process of converting waste materials into new materials and objects. This concept often includes the recovery of energy from waste materials. The recyclability of a material depends on its ability to reacquire the properties it had in its original state. It is an alternative to "conventional" waste disposal that can save material and help lower greenhouse gas emissions. It can al
